entities.name: semaglutide

attributes.formulation: acylated GLP-1 peptide with C18 fatty diacid via gamma-glutamic acid and mini-PEG linker

entities.context: Semaglutide is described as a unique acylated GLP-1 peptide with a half-life of approximately 160 hours (approximately 7 days), prepared per Example 4 of WO2006/097537.

entities.name: liraglutide
entities.context: The long half-life of semaglutide compared to native GLP-1 (half-life approximately 2 minutes) and liraglutide (half-life approximately 13 hours) enables once-weekly dosing.

entities.name: native GLP-1(7-37)
entities.context: The native GLP-1(7-37) peptide sequence is HAEGTFTSDVSSYLEGQAAKEFIAWLVKGRG (SEQ ID NO: 1).

entities.name: GLP-1 receptor agonism
entities.context: A key embodiment involves administering a GLP-1 agonist with a minimum half-life of 72 hours in doses of at least 0.7 mg weekly (or semaglutide-equivalent) on a once-weekly or less frequent schedule.

entities.name: GLP-1 receptor
entities.context: A key embodiment involves administering a GLP-1 agonist with a minimum half-life of 72 hours in doses of at least 0.7 mg weekly (or semaglutide-equivalent) on a once-weekly or less frequent schedule for managing type 2 diabetes, hyperglycemia, glucose tolerance disorders, and obesity.

entities.name: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
entities.context: Clinical studies demonstrated that weekly semaglutide at doses of 0.5 mg and 1.0 mg achieved significant reductions in HbA1c and body weight in patients with type 2 diabetes.

entities.name: Hyperglycemia
entities.context: A key embodiment involves administering a GLP-1 agonist with a minimum half-life of 72 hours in doses of at least 0.7 mg weekly (or semaglutide-equivalent) on a once-weekly or less frequent schedule for managing type 2 diabetes, hyperglycemia, glucose tolerance disorders, and obesity.

entities.name: Obesity
entities.context: A key embodiment involves administering a GLP-1 agonist with a minimum half-life of 72 hours in doses of at least 0.7 mg weekly (or semaglutide-equivalent) on a once-weekly or less frequent schedule for managing type 2 diabetes, hyperglycemia, glucose tolerance disorders, and obesity.

entities.name: SUSTAIN
entities.context: The SUSTAIN clinical trial program showed cardiovascular safety with a trend toward reduced major adverse cardiovascular events (MACE).

entities.name: HbA1c reduction
entities.context: Clinical studies demonstrated that weekly semaglutide at doses of 0.5 mg and 1.0 mg achieved significant reductions in HbA1c and body weight in patients with type 2 diabetes.

entities.name: Major Adverse Cardiovascular Events
entities.context: The SUSTAIN clinical trial program showed cardiovascular safety with a trend toward reduced major adverse cardiovascular events (MACE).

entities.name: US10335462B2
entities.context: Patent ID: US10335462B2, Assignee: Novo Nordisk AS, Filing Date: July 21, 2017

relations.evidence: A key embodiment involves administering a GLP-1 agonist with a minimum half-life of 72 hours in doses of at least 0.7 mg weekly (or semaglutide-equivalent) on a once-weekly or less frequent schedule.

A key embodiment involves administering a GLP-1 agonist with a minimum half-life of 72 hours in doses of at least 0.7 mg weekly (or semaglutide-equivalent) on a once-weekly or less frequent schedule for managing type 2 diabetes, hyperglycemia, glucose tolerance disorders, and obesity.
The native GLP-1(7-37) peptide sequence is HAEGTFTSDVSSYLEGQAAKEFIAWLVKGRG (SEQ ID NO: 1).
Clinical studies demonstrated that weekly semaglutide at doses of 0.5 mg and 1.0 mg achieved significant reductions in HbA1c and body weight in patients with type 2 diabetes.

A key embodiment involves administering a GLP-1 agonist with a minimum half-life of 72 hours in doses of at least 0.7 mg weekly (or semaglutide-equivalent) on a once-weekly or less frequent schedule for managing type 2 diabetes, hyperglycemia, glucose tolerance disorders, and obesity.

A key embodiment involves administering a GLP-1 agonist with a minimum half-life of 72 hours in doses of at least 0.7 mg weekly (or semaglutide-equivalent) on a once-weekly or less frequent schedule for managing type 2 diabetes, hyperglycemia, glucose tolerance disorders, and obesity.

A key embodiment involves administering a GLP-1 agonist with a minimum half-life of 72 hours in doses of at least 0.7 mg weekly (or semaglutide-equivalent) on a once-weekly or less frequent schedule for managing type 2 diabetes, hyperglycemia, glucose tolerance disorders, and obesity.
The SUSTAIN clinical trial program showed cardiovascular safety with a trend toward reduced major adverse cardiovascular events (MACE).

The key semaglutide analog is designated [Aib8, Arg34, Lys37]GLP-1(7-37), denoting substitutions where alpha-aminoisobutyric acid replaces the amino acid at position 8, arginine replaces the amino acid at position 34, and lysine replaces the amino acid at position 37.
Patent ID: US10335462B2, Assignee: Novo Nordisk AS, Filing Date: July 21, 2017
Clinical studies demonstrated that weekly semaglutide at doses of 0.5 mg and 1.0 mg achieved significant reductions in HbA1c and body weight in patients with type 2 diabetes.
The SUSTAIN clinical trial program showed cardiovascular safety with a trend toward reduced major adverse cardiovascular events (MACE).
